# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Load secrets from environment variables or a secret manager rather than embedding them in code.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Store secrets only in platform-provided secure storage or a proper secret manager.
- Minimize persistence of sensitive data and avoid plaintext storage.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not store credentials, tokens, or encryption keys in plaintext application storage.
- Do not hardcode secrets in source code.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;
use Twilio\Rest\Client;
use Twilio\Exceptions\TwilioException;

class TwilioSmsService
{
    /**
     * Twilio REST client instance.
     */
    protected Client $client;

    /**
     * The Twilio phone number to send messages from.
     */
    protected string $fromNumber;

    /**
     * Create a new TwilioSmsService instance.
     *
     * Credentials are loaded from environment variables via Laravel's config system.
     *
     * @throws \InvalidArgumentException If required configuration is missing.
     */
    public function __construct()
    {
        $accountSid = config('services.twilio.account_sid');
        $authToken = config('services.twilio.auth_token');
        $this->fromNumber = config('services.twilio.from_number');

        if (empty($accountSid) || empty($authToken) || empty($this->fromNumber)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException(
                'Twilio credentials are not fully configured. '
                . 'Please set TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, and TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER '
                . 'in your environment variables.'
            );
        }

        $this->client = new Client($accountSid, $authToken);
    }

    /**
     * Send an SMS message to the specified phone number.
     *
     * @param string $to The recipient phone number in E.164 format (e.g., +1234567890).
     * @param string $message The SMS message body.
     * @return array An array containing 'success' (bool), 'message_sid' (string|null), and 'error' (string|null).
     */
    public function sendSms(string $to, string $message): array
    {
        $this->validatePhoneNumber($to);
        $this->validateMessageBody($message);

        try {
            $twilioMessage = $this->client->messages->create(
                $to,
                [
                    'from' => $this->fromNumber,
                    'body' => $message,
                ]
            );

            Log::info('SMS sent successfully.', [
                'to' => $to,
                'message_sid' => $twilioMessage->sid,
                'status' => $twilioMessage->status,
            ]);

            return [
                'success' => true,
                'message_sid' => $twilioMessage->sid,
                'status' => $twilioMessage->status,
                'error' => null,
            ];
        } catch (TwilioException $e) {
            Log::error('Failed to send SMS via Twilio.', [
                'to' => $to,
                'error_code' => $e->getCode(),
                'error_message' => $e->getMessage(),
            ]);

            return [
                'success' => false,
                'message_sid' => null,
                'status' => 'failed',
                'error' => $e->getMessage(),
            ];
        }
    }

    /**
     * Send an SMS message to multiple recipients.
     *
     * @param array $recipients An array of phone numbers in E.164 format.
     * @param string $message The SMS message body.
     * @return array An array of results keyed by phone number.
     */
    public function sendBulkSms(array $recipients, string $message): array
    {
        $results = [];

        foreach ($recipients as $recipient) {
            $results[$recipient] = $this->sendSms($recipient, $message);
        }

        return $results;
    }

    /**
     * Validate that the phone number is in E.164 format.
     *
     * @param string $phoneNumber
     * @throws \InvalidArgumentException
     */
    protected function validatePhoneNumber(string $phoneNumber): void
    {
        if (!preg_match('/^\+[1-9]\d{1,14}$/', $phoneNumber)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException(
                "Invalid phone number format: '{$phoneNumber}'. "
                . 'Phone numbers must be in E.164 format (e.g., +1234567890).'
            );
        }
    }

    /**
     * Validate the SMS message body.
     *
     * @param string $message
     * @throws \InvalidArgumentException
     */
    protected function validateMessageBody(string $message): void
    {
        if (empty(trim($message))) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('SMS message body cannot be empty.');
        }

        if (mb_strlen($message) > 1600) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException(
                'SMS message body exceeds the maximum length of 1600 characters.'
            );
        }
    }

    /**
     * Retrieve the status of a previously sent message.
     *
     * @param string $messageSid The Twilio message SID.
     * @return array An array containing message status details.
     */
    public function getMessageStatus(string $messageSid): array
    {
        try {
            $message = $this->client->messages($messageSid)->fetch();

            return [
                'success' => true,
                'message_sid' => $message->sid,
                'status' => $message->status,
                'to' => $message->to,
                'from' => $message->from,
                'date_sent' => $message->dateSent ? $message->dateSent->format('Y-m-d H:i:s') : null,
                'error_code' => $message->errorCode,
                'error_message' => $message->errorMessage,
            ];
        } catch (TwilioException $e) {
            Log::error('Failed to fetch message status from Twilio.', [
                'message_sid' => $messageSid,
                'error_message' => $e->getMessage(),
            ]);

            return [
                'success' => false,
                'message_sid' => $messageSid,
                'status' => 'unknown',
                'error' => $e->getMessage(),
            ];
        }
    }
}

<?php

// config/services.php (add the following to the existing services configuration array)

return [

    // ... other service configurations ...

    'twilio' => [
        'account_sid' => env('TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID'),
        'auth_token' => env('TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN'),
        'from_number' => env('TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER'),
    ],

];

<?php

// .env.example (add these lines — never commit actual values)
// TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=your_account_sid_here
// TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=your_auth_token_here
// TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER=+1234567890

<?php

namespace App\Providers;

use App\Services\TwilioSmsService;
use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider;

class TwilioServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    /**
     * Register the Twilio SMS service as a singleton in the container.
     */
    public function register(): void
    {
        $this->app->singleton(TwilioSmsService::class, function ($app) {
            return new TwilioSmsService();
        });
    }

    /**
     * Bootstrap any application services.
     */
    public function boot(): void
    {
        //
    }
}
